tier_justification: 1 work-unit (prose-only edit to a single file), no irreversible/outward surface, but no existing test or verify-criterion covers these specific prose claims (the goal state is new/changed text asserting warn-vs-block behaviour) — tier 0's 'existing check already covers it' predicate does not hold, so standard tier 1 applies.

evals.assert: The stale 'every block is a stall' claim about in-loop orchestrator confidence/verify hooks is gone from SKILL.md.
evals.cmd: ! git grep -n "every block is a stall" -- skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md
evals.negative_control: git grep -n "every block is a stall" -- skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md   # passes (finds it) on frozen_sha, proving the grep is capable of matching before the edit

evals.assert: The stale '~8 confidence/verify blocks' past-failure figure is replaced; the real f87a0a2e incident (~69 regenerations / 49 confidence + 20 verify-loop) is now cited in Phase 0.5.
evals.cmd: ! git grep -q "~8 confidence" -- skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md && git grep -q "f87a0a2e" -- skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md && git grep -q "69" -- skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md
evals.negative_control: git grep -q "~8 confidence" -- skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md   # true (matches) on frozen_sha, proving the stale figure is present pre-edit and the grep can detect it

evals.assert: Phase 0.5 opening paragraph states the two discipline hooks WARN (not block) on Stop inside an active loop, and still block outside loops / for worker SubagentStop output.
evals.cmd: git grep -n -A2 "Phase 0.5" -- skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -qi "warn" && git grep -qi "still block\|block outside\|blocks outside\|SubagentStop" -- skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md
evals.negative_control: git grep -n -A2 "Phase 0.5" -- skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -qi "warn"   # exits 1 (no match) on frozen_sha since the opening paragraph only says 'blocks', proving the main check's first clause can fail pre-edit

evals.assert: Both bundle passages (Phase 0.5's LOOP-STOP bullet and the 'Declaring the stop (the LOOP-STOP contract)' section) state that the LOOP-STOP declaration line is the FINAL line of the stopping turn, and the bundling rationale is extended for the warn era (in-loop discipline enforcement is warn-level for the orchestrator, so bundling keeps confidence-label + DNV present for post-hoc transcript audit).
evals.verifier_instruction: You are grading a prose edit to skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md in the coderails repo, on branch docs/loop-stop-bundle-warn-era, against origin/main. You have NOT seen any implementation conversation — grade only from the file content and this instruction. Read the merged/current skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md. Find (a) the Phase 0.5 section's LOOP-STOP bullet (originally near 'End any stopping turn inside an active loop with a LOOP-STOP declaration line') and (b) the 'Declaring the stop (the LOOP-STOP contract)' section near the end of the file. PASS requires ALL of: (1) both passages explicitly state the LOOP-STOP line must be the LAST/FINAL line of the stopping turn (not merely 'in the same turn' — ordering must be explicit in both places); (2) the rationale for bundling LOOP-STOP with confidence-label/DNV is tied to the warn-era reality — i.e. states or clearly implies that because in-loop Stop-event enforcement of the two discipline hooks is now warn-level (not block) for the orchestrator, bundling is what keeps confidence labels and Did-Not-Verify content present in the transcript for later audit, rather than being framed purely around 'avoid clearing one hook only to trip another'; (3) the two passages are mutually consistent (neither contradicts the other on ordering or rationale); (4) neither passage claims the two discipline hooks still block in-loop orchestrator Stop-event turns. FAIL if any of these is missing, or if the ordering/rationale claim is only implied by keyword presence without making the actual rule clear to a reader. Report PASS or FAIL per criterion (1)-(4) with a one-line quote as evidence for each, then an overall PASS/FAIL.

evals.assert: No other passage in SKILL.md still claims the confidence-label or verify-loop hooks block in-loop orchestrator Stop turns (self-check sweep) — loop_stall_guard/loop_state_guard block-claims are untouched and excluded from this check.
evals.cmd: ! git grep -niE "confidence-label (block|blocks)|verify-loop (block|blocks)|confidence/verify block" -- skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -v "[0-9] confidence-label blocks\|[0-9] verify-loop blocks"
evals.negative_control: git grep -niE "confidence-label (block|blocks)|verify-loop (block|blocks)|confidence/verify block" -- skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -v "[0-9] confidence-label blocks\|[0-9] verify-loop blocks"   # matches on frozen_sha (line 132's 'confidence-label and verify-loop blocks', not a counted historical figure), proving the sweep can fail pre-edit

evals.assert: The PR diff touches exactly skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md and nothing else (manifest scope assertion).
evals.negative_control: git diff origin/main --name-only   # on frozen_sha (no diff yet), this is empty, which fails the exact-match test against the required filename, proving the check can fail

amendments.why: Original regex flagged 'confidence-label blocks'/'verify-loop blocks' as bare phrases, which false-positived on the new past-failure sentence's historical count ('49 confidence-label blocks + 20 verify-loop blocks') — accurate historical data about the f87a0a2e incident, explicitly framed as the motivating cause of the demotion, not a stale present-tense claim. Narrowed the sweep to exclude the counted-historical-figure pattern while keeping it sensitive to any present-tense stale claim. Made pre-grading (no verifier verdict existed yet), so no regraded_by required.
